summary_one_line: Input must drive a protocol-completion transfer with a parsed Content-Length (k->size != -1) and a body bytecount short of that size.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structured HTTP-protocol request: a scheme/URL prefix (e.g. pop:// or ://127.0.0.1:9001) plus an HTTP response carrying the literal tokens HTTP/ and a Content-Length-style header, so curl parses a non-(-1) k->size and then sees the connection close with k->bytecount != k->size, taking the true side of the line-1303 partial-file check.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds embed exact protocol literals — pop, ://, the host:port 127.0.0.1:9001/850, GHTTP/ and a Content header string — at fixed offsets, so curl advances through MSTATE_PERFORMING into the transfer-completion else-branch with a known size and a short body, hitting the blocker true side (W T=6 F=0 at L1303).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short (35-73 bytes) and dominated by repeated %, ., +, A filler with no coherent scheme/HTTP/Content-Length structure, so they never reach the completion path with a parsed size and stay on the false side (L T=0 F=20 at L1303).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the per-execution CmpLogObserver buffer of operands compared at curl's protocol/header CMP sites and substitutes the expected literal (HTTP/, Content-Length, scheme tokens) at matching input offsets; naive and value_profile lack this mutator and cannot synthesize the multi-byte protocol literals by chance, so only the I2S-equipped arm of each pair resolves the branch.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side at transfer.c:1303 is the partial-file completion check guarded by k->size != -1.
Both decisive pairs have the I2S technique delta and the I2S-equipped fuzzer wins 10/10 while the loser blocks 10/10.
The second decisive pair is the value_profile_cmplog over value_profile I2S contrast.
Winner-resolving seeds carry coherent protocol literals: a scheme prefix, host:port, HTTP/, and a Content header string.
A winning seed embeds the host:port URL pattern 127.0.0.1:9001 used by curl's parser.
A winning seed carries the literal Content header token in its tail bytes.
Loser-blocking seeds are short and dominated by repeated filler bytes with no protocol structure.
At L1303 the winner takes the true side while the loser stays on the false side.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for byte sequences matching one CMP operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator stage.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 459 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed a608095a4d425f1c` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 28 and 29 of the 30-level ancestor chain — direct lower-bound evidence of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in this cmplog winner's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness reproducing the line-1303 size/bytecount-mismatch gate where cmplog and naive resolve the true side at statistically indistinguishable rates, showing the protocol literals are reachable without I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP site whose operand I2S substitutes (HTTP/ version token vs Content-Length header vs scheme) is inferred from seed byte content, not directly tied to a named comparison in the source overlay.
